text.title: Prompt Version Pinning Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that defeat prompt version pinning in production. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Silent version drift — engineer edits a prompt in the registry without updating the lockfile, the gateway picks up the new content under the old version ID, replay/debugging falsely matches the wrong content.
failure-mode.detector: content hash of prompts/registry/{prompt_id}/{version}/prompt.txt differs from the hash recorded in lockfile history
failure-mode.repair: Registry contents are immutable; pre-commit hook computes hashes and rejects any modification to an already-published version. The repair for past drift is to retire the affected version and create a new immutable one with a corrected ID.

failure-mode.description: Unpinned client call — a client SDK bypasses the version field, the gateway is misconfigured to allow unpinned calls in prod, traffic silently hits a "latest" alias.
failure-mode.detector: gateway audit log shows calls without an explicit version OR gateway_policy.reject_unpinned = false in prod
failure-mode.repair: Set gateway_policy.reject_unpinned = true in prod immediately. Audit the call sites; force every client SDK to include the version in the request. Add an integration test that the gateway returns HTTP 400 on unpinned prod calls.

failure-mode.description: Rollback stuck — the 60-second rollback contract is violated because the gateway requires a deploy or cache-bust to load the lockfile change.
failure-mode.detector: rollback_receipt.completed_within_seconds > 60 OR rollback path requires a CI pipeline run
failure-mode.repair: Redesign the gateway to hot-reload the lockfile (push notification, file watcher, or in-memory store). The rollback CLI must be atomic and not require a deploy. Re-run the quarterly game-day to verify.

failure-mode.description: Canary-skipped full rollout — engineer ships full rollout without a canary, eval delta misses a regression because the eval suite did not catch it, customer quality drops.
failure-mode.detector: lockfile shows rollout_strategy=full AND no canary entry in this prompt's history within 30 days
failure-mode.repair: Block full rollouts at the gateway-policy level for any prompt that has not been in canary for a minimum 24h hold. Override requires written ML-lead approval logged in the lockfile metadata.

failure-mode.description: A/B comparison drifts past 4 weeks — experiment never closes, both arms live in production indefinitely, gateway audit logs become impossible to interpret.
failure-mode.detector: lockfile has an active rollout_percent split that has not converged to 0 or 100 within 30 days of opening
failure-mode.repair: Force the close: if statistical significance is not reached by the 4-week mark, ML lead makes a Bayesian-prior decision based on the data so far AND closes the split. The losing arm is retired; the winning arm goes to full rollout.

failure-mode.description: Lockfile hand-edited — engineer edits prompts.lock.yaml directly instead of using the ops CLI, history entries become inconsistent.
failure-mode.detector: commit to prompts.lock.yaml from an author other than the ops CLI bot OR commit not signed by the ops-CLI key
failure-mode.repair: Require ops-CLI bot as the sole author of lockfile commits. Pre-commit hook on the lockfile checks the signing identity. Hand-edits are reverted; the engineer re-runs the change through the CLI.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Vendor / provider pressure to "just try this new prompt directly in production for a quick demo" must NOT bypass the gateway pinning. The runbook's value compounds with discipline; one exception trains the team that the gateway is bypass-able.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly: run a rollback game day. Simulate a regression, execute the rollback CLI, time it. If the rollback takes > 60 seconds, schedule the engineering work to fix the gateway. The rollback contract is the most consequential property of the runbook; it must work when it is needed.
